evals.prompt: I just ran the software-archeologist skill using claude-sonnet-4-6 with 1200 input tokens and 450 output tokens. Emit the appropriate telemetry event.
evals.expected_output: Agent calls send_event with event_type='skill.invoked', skill_name='software-archeologist', model='claude-sonnet-4-6', input_tokens=1200, output_tokens=450, provider='anthropic', and estimated_cost_usd calculated from cost_rates table (non-null).

assertions.text: Output contains send_event call or @skill_span usage with event_type='skill.invoked'
provider='anthropic' is inferred from model name

evals.prompt: Generate a cost report for the last 30 days grouped by model.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes `cornerstone report cost --group-by model` or calls GET /v1/summary. Does NOT attempt to read local files for cost data.

assertions.text: Agent uses CLI or HTTP API, not local file reads
Output includes model names and USD amounts

evals.prompt: I just created a new skill at .agents/skills/software/quality/performance-reviewer/SKILL.md. Record this as a knowledge creation event.

assertions.text: path matches the provided skill path

evals.prompt: AGENTIC_TELEMETRY_URL is not set. What happens when I call send_event?
evals.expected_output: Agent confirms send_event is a complete no-op — no network call, no exception, no log output. Execution continues normally.

assertions.text: Response confirms no-op behavior when env var is absent
Response does NOT suggest the agent should skip telemetry calls — they should always be present in code
